Warm Up 2: Slot Machine Research  
Owen Atis (A18214213)

Visual Design

* Colors:   
  * Machines featuring high-contrast color schemes maintain player attention 23% longer than muted color pallets. Warm colors also show higher rates of repeated play compared to cooler tones.   
  * Psychological Impacts of specific color choices to player behavior:   
    * Red: Usually correlates to increased engagement and decision-making patterns. (IE. Rerolling or making new bets after a loss).   
    * Blue: Measurable effects on session duration, linger play times.   
    * Gold & Yellow: Ranks highest in player preference surveys when associated w/ reward indicators and win celebrations.   
    * Green: positive associations with player confidence, especially in interactive components like buttons (spinning, selecting bet increases/decreases, etc.).   
  * Cultural Elements:   
    * The use of cultural Elements in theming can maintain player engagement by 40%.   
    * Example: Asian Markets  
* Change:   
  * Features of Wizards, Horse Races, Loud Music, Game Show Characters

Sound Design

* Victory Indicators: Increases in player satisfaction and engagement.   
* Interactive Feedback: Response times under 50 milliseconds between button presses and sound create optimal player experience.   
* Ambient Audio: Background music matching cultural preferences increases average play session durations by 15%. 

Game Mechanics: 

* Variable Rewards:   
  * Unpredictable reward patterns create more sustained engagement than fixed-ratio systems.   
* Near-miss events trigger dopamine responses similar to actual wins.   
* \# of reels: Three or More symbols that can vary in matching.   
* Odds favor the house, players are likely to walk away with less money than when they started. (House advantage average is 8%). 

Gamification: 

* Levels and Progression: The more a player plays the more features and bonuses they unlock.   
* Missions and Challenges: Slots may ask players to hit certain combinations or play for a set time to earn rewards.   
* Storylines: Slots could have a narrative that entices players to play to see more.   
* Social Elements: Collaboration with others allows for more immersion and interaction while grouping more players.   
* Psychology (The Why)  
  * Dopamine Rush: Every win, even small ones entice a player to keep winning.  
  * Near-Miss Effect: Almost winning can be as exciting as winning.  
  * Power of Progress: Progression makes players feel they're accomplishing something even if it isn't monetary.   
  * Illusion of Control  
* Trends:   
  * Virtual Reality Slots  
  * Skill-Based Bonus Rounds  
  * Personalized Experiences  
  * Collaborative Play

Strict Regulations

* [Responsible Gaming Code of Conduct](https://www.americangaming.org/member-code-of-conduct/)  
* Prevent Underage Gambling   
  * Communicate the legal age to gamble through messaging.   
  * If a child is in violation, proper personnel are to be contacted.   
* Advertise Responsibly   
  * NO false advertising or misleading claims  
  * No images, symbols, celebrity endorsements or language that are designed to appeal specifically to children and minors.   
  * No suggestions or implications of illegal practices.   
  * No promises of financial or personal successes being a guarantee. 

UIGEA

* The Unlawful Internet Gambling Enforcement Act  
* Payment Regulation: Banks and credit card companies should block illegal online gambling transactions. Transparency of financial records became a requirement of online casinos and gambling companies.   
* Penalties: Violation penalties (fines, imprisonment, asset forfeiture).   
* Scope and Control: Individual states decide on internet gambling regulations. 

The Users:

* Advantage play: Players doing specific strategies in an attempt to increase expected winnings.  
* Sour rooters: Individuals who loiter near slot machines in hopes of taking up a machine when it is “ready” to hit a jackpot.   
* Four Groups of Players:   
  * Utilitarian:   
    * People looking for something to do.  
    * Senior Citizens looking to kill time, reduce boredom or get out of the house, less motivated by financial rewards.   
  * Excitement  
    * Looking for the thrill of winning a jackpot, relaxing and having a good time.   
    * Not a fan of progressive machines w/ bigger payouts.   
  * Multipurpose  
    * Younger, Less educated, lower income and less likely to be marred and own a home.   
    * Motivated by fun and winning money, attracted to themed games and other machines considered “lucky”  
  * Relaxation   
    * Used for socialization and fun.   
    * Stay within denomination usually of 25-cent to $1 machines to relieve day-to-day stress.   
  * Gender Differences:   
    * Women for the social experience, excitement and fun.   
    * Men for the financial rewards.

Sources: 

* [The Science Behind Slot Machine Design \- Heartwood college of Art](https://www.heartwoodcollegeofart.org/the-science-behind-slot-machine-design/)  
* [How Slots Work \- GameSense](https://www.gamesense.com/games/how-do-slot-machines-work.html#:~:text=Slot%20machines%20contain%20random%20number,matches%20a%20payline%2C%20you%20win.)  
* [The Psychology of Slot Machine Gamification \- smartico](https://www.smartico.ai/blog-post/psychology-slot-machine-gamification)  
* [Responsible Gaming Regulations and Statutes Guide \- American Gaming Association](http://americangaming.org/resources/responsible-gaming-regulations-and-statutes-guide/)  
* [Understanding Online Gambling Laws in the United States in 2026 \- Fortis Media](https://www.fortismedia.com/en/articles/online-gambling-laws/)  
* [Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/vegas/comments/1mutaos/slot_machines_what_are_the_lurkers_looking_for/)  
* [Study finds slot machine players don’t fit stereotype \- Oregon State University](https://news.oregonstate.edu/news/study-finds-slot-machine-players-dont-fit-stereotype)
